description: US medication access for uninsured and underinsured: patient assistance programs, $4 generics, insulin cost caps, 340B clinics, manufacturer programs. Verified 2026.
source: NeedyMeds, RxAssist, HRSA 340B program guidance, IRA 2022 insulin cap provisions, FDA generic drug guidance

triples.subject: patient assistance program (PAP)
triples.object: free or low-cost brand-name medications provided by pharmaceutical manufacturers to uninsured or underinsured patients

triples.subject: patient assistance program
triples.object: uninsured or underinsured, income usually under 200–400% FPL; some programs have no income cap

triples.subject: patient assistance program
triples.object: NeedyMeds.org, RxAssist.org, or manufacturer website directly — search drug name + 'patient assistance'

triples.subject: patient assistance program
triples.object: doctor's office often submits applications on behalf of patients; ask your prescriber for help

triples.subject: $4 generics
triples.object: retail pharmacy programs offering common generics for $4/month or $10/90-day supply without insurance

triples.subject: $4 generics
triples.object: Walmart Rx $4 list, Kroger Rx Savings Club, Publix free antibiotic/prenatal program, Wegmans free generics

triples.subject: $4 generics
triples.object: covers many common drugs: metformin, lisinopril, atorvastatin, amoxicillin, levothyroxine, SSRIs, others

triples.subject: GoodRx
triples.object: free coupon/discount program reducing retail drug prices — often cheaper than insurance copay

triples.subject: GoodRx
triples.object: cannot be combined with insurance; ask pharmacist to run GoodRx price vs. insurance to find lowest

triples.subject: Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs
triples.object: online pharmacy selling generics at cost plus 15% markup and $5 pharmacy fee — often dramatically cheaper

triples.subject: Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs
triples.object: costplusdrugs.com — requires a valid prescription; ships to most states

triples.subject: insulin cost cap Medicare
triples.object: Inflation Reduction Act 2022 caps Medicare Part D insulin at $35/month per insulin product

triples.subject: insulin cost cap Medicare
triples.object: Medicare beneficiaries — caps apply to covered insulins under Part D and Part B since January 2023

triples.subject: insulin manufacturer programs
triples.object: Lilly Insulin Value Program caps out-of-pocket at $35/month for uninsured; $35 co-pay cards for insured

triples.subject: insulin manufacturer programs
triples.object: Patient Assistance Program for uninsured; My$99Insulin program for cash-pay patients

triples.subject: insulin manufacturer programs
triples.object: Insulins Valyou Savings Program; PAP for low-income uninsured; insulinhelp.com

triples.subject: 340B program
triples.object: federal program requiring drug manufacturers to provide discounted medications to qualifying healthcare organizations

triples.subject: 340B program
triples.object: Section 340B of the Public Health Service Act, 42 U.S.C. § 256b

triples.subject: 340B clinic
triples.object: Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs), Ryan White HIV clinics, disproportionate share hospitals, tribal clinics

triples.subject: 340B clinic
triples.object: clinic buys drugs at 340B discount and may pass savings to uninsured/underinsured patients

triples.subject: 340B clinic
triples.object: HRSA Health Center Finder: findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov — FQHCs serve patients regardless of ability to pay

triples.subject: FQHC sliding scale
triples.object: Federally Qualified Health Centers charge on sliding fee scale based on income; no one turned away for inability to pay

triples.subject: NeedyMeds
triples.object: database of PAPs, free clinics, disease-specific assistance programs, drug discount cards

triples.subject: NeedyMeds
triples.object: needymeds.org or call 800-503-6897

triples.subject: RxOutreach
triples.object: nonprofit mail-order pharmacy offering generic medications at low cost to income-eligible patients

triples.subject: Extra Help / LIS
triples.object: Medicare Low Income Subsidy (LIS) covers most Part D premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income enrollees

triples.subject: Extra Help / LIS
triples.object: income under ~150% FPL, limited resources; automatically enrolled if receiving SSI or full Medicaid

triples.subject: Extra Help / LIS
triples.object: Social Security Administration — ssa.gov/extrahelp or 1-800-772-1213

triples.subject: state pharmaceutical assistance programs
triples.object: Pennsylvania PACE/PACENET, New York EPIC, New Jersey PAAD — many states have senior drug assistance

triples.subject: medication splitting
triples.object: for some medications, prescribing double-dose tablets cut in half can halve cost — ask prescriber if tablet is splittable
